task_completion.description: Navigate to the location of Meteor Crater (Barringer Crater) in Arizona.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The application viewport displays a satellite or map view of a distinct circular crater. If coordinates are visible in the status bar, they should be near 35.028°N, 111.022°W.

task_completion.description: Trace the crater rim with a polygon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A polygon overlay is visible on the map, closely following the circular rim of the crater geometry.
A properties dialog, sidebar entry, or map label associated with the polygon displays the text 'Barringer Crater Rim'.

task_completion.description: Add a description noting it is an impact crater.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A properties or description field is visible containing text that includes the words 'impact crater'.

task_completion.description: Apply a semi-transparent fill to the polygon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The polygon's fill color is visible but allows the underlying terrain or satellite imagery to be seen through it (opacity < 100%).
A 'Save As' dialog, file manager, or export confirmation shows the filename 'meteor_crater.kml' being saved to the '/home/ga/Documents' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a GIS application GUI (e.g., Marble, Google Earth).
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the interface of a mapping application with map controls, toolbars, and rendering, rather than a text editor or IDE.

integrity.description: Verify the agent manually drew the polygon using application tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of the polygon tool being active, vertices being placed via mouse clicks, or properties being edited in the GUI, rather than importing a pre-made file.

integrity.description: Verify the output was not generated via terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is not observed typing KML/XML code directly into a terminal or script to generate the file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Meteor Crater (Barringer Crater) is located at 35.028°N, 111.022°W. It is a circular impact crater with a diameter of approximately 1.2 km.
